text.title: typescript-patterns Procedure
text.summary: Five-step procedure: domain glossary → spec → validate.

step.name: enumerate-types
step.action: List every domain concept that needs a type (entities, IDs, value objects, request/response shapes, errors).

step.name: pick-representation
step.action: For each: external-input → zod-schema; internal entity → interface; ID-of-string → branded-string; nullable/either result → discriminated-union.
step.output: List with representation set for each.

step.name: declare-validators
step.action: Decide between zod-parse (throws on invalid; suitable for trusted-program errors) and zod-safe-parse (returns a Result; suitable for user-facing input).
step.output: validation field set for every external-input type.
step.decision-gate: Reject any external-input type with validation in {none, type-guard} — these are insufficient for trust-boundary input.

step.name: brand-ids
step.input: All ID-like types that share an underlying primitive.
step.action: Mark them representation="branded-string" / "branded-number"; declare factory function name in the spec consumer's notes.
